cards.text: Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 39% in the first ten months of 2025
cards.sub: The collapse predates Trump-Xi. Plantation Minister Johari told MPOC in November the cause was price (Malaysian CPO at RM4,200 a tonne, USD120 above soybean oil), not the new summit headlines.
sources: MPOC, 27 Nov 2025 — Johari: Drop in Palm Oil Exports to China Due to Price, Not Geopolitics
The Edge / MPOB monthly release, 12 May 2026 — April 2026 palm oil stocks and exports

cards.h: When CPO sat USD120 above soybean oil in 2025, Chinese buyers switched
cards.s: Malaysia's 2024 exports to China were 1.39 million tonnes, down 5.3% on 2023. By October 2025 the cumulative year-to-date drop reached 39%. Price spread, not summit politics.
cards.d: Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani told the Malaysian Palm Oil Council on 27 November 2025 that the cause of the 39% drop in Malaysia's China exports over the first ten months of 2025 was price. Malaysian crude palm oil at one point traded at a USD120 per tonne premium to soybean oil during the year. China's vegetable oil consumption is structurally weighted toward soybean oil (44%) and rapeseed oil (24%), with palm oil at 18%; when the palm-soy spread inverts, Chinese refiners shift mix. The 2024 baseline of 1.39 million tonnes was already a 5.3% decline from 2023 per MPOB-derived trade data, so the 39% Jan-Oct 2025 collapse is a deepening, not a sudden break.
sources: MPOC, 27 Nov 2025 — Johari statement (primary, attributed cabinet minister)
TradeImex 2024-25 export review citing MPOB-derived destination data
AOCS — China's evolving edible oils industry (consumption mix)

cards.h: China signed a US$10 billion oilseed deal with Brazil in November 2025
cards.s: COFCO bought roughly 20 million tonnes of soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil from ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus at the Shanghai Import Expo. That competing supply is already booked.
cards.d: China's state oilseed trader COFCO signed multi-billion-dollar agreements at the China International Import Expo in Shanghai in November 2025 to buy approximately 20 million tonnes of Brazilian soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil, with a total contract value above US$10 billion. The counterparties were the four largest global agri-traders: ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus. Separately, Brazil-China trade in soybeans accelerated through 2025 as US tariff frictions cut US soybean exports to China by roughly 75% from the prior year (Soygrowers paper, August 2025). The Trump-Xi May 2026 deal is therefore not bringing fresh oilseed supply into a vacuum, it is competing against capacity China has already contracted.
sources: Datamarnews / MarketScreener — COFCO Brazilian deal announcement, November 2025
Farm Policy News, University of Illinois — China buys more Brazil soybeans as US purchases stall, November 2025
American Soybean Association — Soybeans Without a Buyer policy paper, August 2025 (primary)

cards.h: China has not formally committed. Soybean traders sold after the announcement
cards.s: USTR Greer told Bloomberg China would buy 'double-digit billions per year for three years.' Chinese FM Wang Yi said working teams are still negotiating. July soybean futures fell 36 cents a bushel that day.
cards.d: On 15 May 2026 US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg that China was expected to commit to double-digit billion dollar agricultural purchases over the next three years, per year, covering soybeans, corn, sorghum, milling wheat, beef and poultry. The same day, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said working teams from both countries were still negotiating the relevant details. Market reaction was negative: July soybean futures dropped more than 36 cents per bushel on lack of specifics. Boeing, separately included in the deal, announced an order for 200 aircraft, less than half the 500 figure Trump had floated before the summit; Boeing shares fell 4%. The October 2025 prior commitment of 25 million metric tonnes of US soybeans annually through 2028 remains on paper, with zero new-crop export orders booked for the 2025/26 marketing year as of the summit date.
sources: Bloomberg, 15 May 2026 — US Expects Dramatic Agricultural Purchases by China, Greer Says (primary, USTR-attributed)
DTN Progressive Farmer — USTR: China Pledges to Buy Billions, 15 May 2026
Al Jazeera — Day 1 Beijing talks coverage with Wang Yi statement
Yahoo Finance / Reuters — July soybean futures reaction
Atlantic Council / CNBC — Boeing 200-jet order vs 500 floated
American Soybean Association — zero new-crop export orders MY 2025/26

cards.text: The story is not Trump shaking Malaysian palm oil. It is Malaysian palm oil losing China to soybean oil, Brazil and Indonesia, a process two years entrenched before the cameras showed up in Beijing.

cards.h: Think of it like booking a refund for damage already paid out
cards.text: The dam broke in 2024. By the time the Trump-Xi rain arrived in May 2026, the field was already flooded, and another upstream dam (Brazil's COFCO deal) was already diverting the water.

Mainstream framing puts the Trump-Xi summit at the centre of any threat to Malaysian palm oil. The evidence puts it at the margin. Demand from China collapsed before the summit, primarily on price. Brazil's COFCO deal already locked in US$10 billion of competing oilseed supply in November 2025. Indonesia is absorbing 15.6 billion litres of its own palm oil into the B40 biodiesel mandate this year, with B50 planned for late 2026. Plantation Minister Johari already named the cause publicly. The honest read is that the summit confirms a story already running, and the more telling Malaysian numbers are the ones markets were already pricing: Bursa CPO under RM4,450 a tonne on summit day, total palm oil exports down 14.34% in April 2026.

sources: USTR / Bloomberg (Greer, 15 May 2026); DTN Progressive Farmer; Reuters via Yahoo Finance; MPOC (Minister Johari, 27 Nov 2025); MPOB monthly statistical release April 2026 (via The Edge Malaysia and The Star); American Soybean Association policy paper (Aug 2025); Farm Policy News / University of Illinois (Nov 2025); Datamarnews and MarketScreener on COFCO Brazil deal (Nov 2025); Farmdoc Daily (Nov 2025); Science Direct peer-reviewed paper on US-China trade war palm-oil substitution; USDA FAS Indonesia Oilseeds Update; Business-Indonesia.org B40 mandate; AOCS — China's evolving edible oils industry; Al Jazeera Day 1 Beijing summit coverage; CNN Politics live blog; CNBC summit takeaways; Atlantic Council analyst note; CFR media briefing; Bank Negara Malaysia LCSF infosheet; Malay Mail, The Star, FMT on Anwar RM82.1B local-currency trade; Palm Oil Magazine on Bursa CPO daily price; The Edge Malaysia and The Star on MPOB April 2026 release; EU Commission EUDR page and Sarawak Tribune on Dec 2026 implementation.
confidence: High — the 39% China-export drop figure is directly attributed to Plantation Minister Johari Abdul Ghani via the Malaysian Palm Oil Council (industry-promotion body under his own ministry's portfolio), making it the strongest possible Malaysian-source for this finding. Greer's 'double-digit billions per year for three years' phrasing is directly quoted by Bloomberg, DTN PF and Reuters from the same USTR interview. The COFCO-Brazil deal volume (20 million tonnes) and value (>US$10 billion) is reported consistently across Datamarnews, MarketScreener and Farm Policy News, with named counterparties (ADM, Bunge, Cargill, Louis Dreyfus) corroborated. The 1.39 million tonnes 2024 figure and 5.3% decline are MPOB-derived. Bursa CPO daily levels (FCPO under RM4,450 on 14 May 2026; 14.34% MoM drop in April 2026 total exports) are from MPOB monthly release. Indonesia B40 figures (15.6 billion litres) from USDA FAS and Business-Indonesia.org.
needs_verification: Whether the Malaysian government (Anwar / MITI / Plantation Ministry) has made any formal public response to the specific Greer / Trump-Xi agriculture deal framing as of 16 May 2026. The brief found no such response in the radar accumulated data; if one has been issued in the last 24-48 hours, the cards may need an explicit MY-government-says line.

Whether the October 2025 US-Malaysia Reciprocal Trade Agreement signed between Anwar and Trump at the ASEAN Kuala Lumpur summit contains any clauses linking Malaysian palm oil treatment to broader US-China bilateral commitments. FMT reporting indicates scope/review mechanism is opaque per think-tank analysis; primary text has not been verified.

Current palm-vs-soybean-oil price spread as of 16 May 2026. Sources cited reflect peak premium ($120/t in 2025) and a recent discount of ~$50/t. The exact spot on summit day should be confirmed against Mysteel daily margin survey for the cleanest framing of the present-tense substitution dynamic.

Whether any major Malaysian palm-oil plantation listco (Sime Darby, IOI, KLK) has issued an investor-facing assessment of the Trump-Xi deal impact. This would strengthen the 'markets already priced this' line in the view card.
Whether the EU EUDR delay to 30 December 2026 has been formally gazetted in the EU Official Journal or remains a Commission statement only.
source_notes: Five known source discrepancies disclosed. (1) US soybean export drop to China: Science Direct peer-reviewed paper uses ~75%; American Soybean Association August 2025 paper gives ~78% derived from bushel-level USDA data (~218M vs ~1B bushels). The cards use the conservative ~75% framing. (2) Boeing aircraft commitment: 200 was the announced order; 500 was Trump's pre-summit floated figure; 150 was Boeing's analyst expectation. Cards use the announced 200. (3) Greer's 'double-digit billions' is aggregate annual across all products (soybeans + corn + sorghum + wheat + beef + poultry), not soybeans alone; popular framing often elides this. Card 3 preserves Greer's full product list in the deep text. (4) China's acknowledgment: Trump claims the deal is agreed (Fox News); Wang Yi says still negotiating. Card 3 surfaces this contradiction directly. (5) Palm-vs-soybean spread direction reversed during 2025: palm at a USD120 premium at peak in 2025 (Johari's frame), at a USD50 discount by late 2025 / early 2026 (Grainfuel Nexus and Mysteel-derived). The hook card uses the 2025-peak frame because that is what Johari publicly named as the cause; the brief flagged this; Stage 3 should be aware that 'price' has reversed sign since Johari's statement, which is a substantive 30-day clarification to surface in synthesis.
